author: Sofia Martínez
title: Social Media AI Content That Doesn't Sound Like a Robot Wrote It
description: AI-generated social content has a reputation for sounding generic. Here's the workflow that produces brand-specific posts your audience actually engages with.

sections.p: The biggest failure mode of AI social media content isn't that it's wrong — it's that it sounds like everyone else's AI content. Generic value props, hollow enthusiasm, hashtag soup. The businesses whose AI content actually performs have solved the personalization problem, not just the volume problem.

sections.h2: The brand voice system that makes AI content authentic

sections.p: Before generating a single post, we build a brand voice document for each client: specific phrases they use and avoid, the tone they take on different topic types, examples of their best-performing past posts, and the persona they're speaking to. That document becomes the system prompt for every AI content generation request.

With a detailed brand voice input, Claude produces posts that sound like the business — specific, opinionated, in the right register. Without it, every AI tool produces the same generic business-page content. The document is the differentiator.

sections.h2: The content mix that drives engagement

list: Educational (40%): practical tips and process insights that prove expertise without pitching.
Social proof (25%): client results, reviews, and before/after content.
Behind the scenes (20%): team, process, and 'day in the life' content that builds trust.
Promotional (15%): offers, launches, and direct CTAs — kept at minority share to maintain audience trust.

sections.h2: The production workflow we run for clients

sections.p: Monday: AI generates 14 post drafts based on the content calendar brief — enough for 2 per day for the week. Tuesday: human review selects, edits, and approves 10 posts. Wednesday–Friday: scheduled posts go live; engagement is monitored and top-performing themes are noted for the next week's brief. Total human time: 90 minutes per week.

sections.h2: What AI can't replace in social media

sections.p: Real-time responsiveness — commenting on a local news story, responding to a trending conversation — requires a human. Genuine relationship-building in the comments requires a human. Crisis communication requires a human. AI handles the content production layer so humans can focus on the relationship layer, which is where social media actually generates business.

sections.quote: AI writes the posts. Humans build the relationships. Both are necessary. Neither alone is sufficient.
